chunks.text: Course: CS 84 | EECS at UC Berkeley CS 84. Sophomore Seminar Catalog Description: Sophomore seminars are small interactive courses offered by faculty members in departments all across the campus. Sophomore seminars offer opportunity for close, regular intellectual contact between faculty members and students in the crucial second year. The topics vary from department to department and semester to semester. Enrollment limited to 15 sophomores. Units: 1-2 Also Offered As: COMPSCI 84 Prerequisites: At discretion of instructor. Formats: Fall: 1.5-3.0 hours of seminar per week Fall: 1.0-2.0 hours of seminar per week Spring: 1.0-2.0 hours of seminar per week Summer: 2.0-4.0 hours of seminar per week Spring: 3.0-6.0 hours of seminar per week Fall: 3.0-6.0 hours of seminar per week Summer: 2.5-5.0 hours of seminar per week Spring: 1.5-3.0 hours of seminar per week Grading Basis: Determined by Instructor Final Exam Status: Yes Links: EECS Course Archives (CalNet authentication required) CS enrollment policies EE enrollment policies HKN course maps
